dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.connectHint: Connect your account to see trip data
dashboard.connectHintDot: Connect your Tesla account to see trip data.
dashboard.connectTooltip: Redirects to the Tesla sign-in page. After approval, your vehicle is detected automatically.
dashboard.totalKmTooltip: Sum of all recorded trip kilometres for the currently selected vehicle. Click: show all trips.
dashboard.tripsTooltip: Number of trips auto-detected by the system (each trip park-to-park). Click: trip list.
dashboard.chargedTooltip: Total energy charged across all sessions. Click: charging statistics.
dashboard.chargingCostTooltip: Summed cost of all charging sessions with a configured price. Free or unknown charges count as 0 €. Click: charging statistics.
dashboard.monthlyKmTooltip: Kilometres driven per month – shows your mobility patterns and seasonal differences
dashboard.fromTooltip: Start address or GPS coordinates of the most recent trip
dashboard.toTooltip: End address or GPS coordinates of the most recent trip
dashboard.distanceTooltip: Distance of the last trip in kilometres
dashboard.consumptionTooltip: Energy consumption per 100 km – lower values mean efficient driving. Tesla average: 15–20 kWh/100km

trips.title: Trips
trips.purpose: Enter trip purpose (e.g. client visit, business trip)…
trips.heatmapTooltip: Calendar heatmap of all trips. Hover for details, click navigates to that day's trip list.
trips.totalKmTooltip: Sum of all recorded trip kilometres
trips.wltpTooltip: Deviation from your model's WLTP value. + = more consumed, − = less.

webhooks.intro: Send app events (trip end, charging end, service due) to your own HTTP endpoints. Every POST is HMAC-SHA256 signed; verify the {signatureHeader} header against the secret shown once on creation.
webhooks.secretHint: Copy the secret now; it will not be shown again. Use it to verify the HMAC signature.

chargingHeatmap.intro: When and how fast do you charge? Brighter = more often / more power. Hover a cell for details.
chargingHeatmap.hoverDetail: {sessions} session(s), ø {avg} kW, {kwh} kWh, peak {peak} kW

locationHeatmap.intro: Where do you drive often? Bright areas = visited often. Hover/click on the map.

annualReport.title: Tesla Carview · Annual report
annualReport.footer: Generated with Tesla Carview — Self-hosted, your data

tripDetail.title: Trip detail
tripDetail.noGps: No GPS data available for this trip.

charging.title: Charging
charging.sessionsTooltip: Number of all auto-detected charging sessions – from plug-in to plug-out.
charging.totalChargedTooltip: Sum of recharged energy in kilowatt-hours across all sessions.
charging.totalCostTooltip: Summed cost of all sessions with a configured rate. Free charges count as 0 €.
charging.maxPowerTooltip: Highest charging power ever measured. Tesla Model 3/Y: up to 250 kW at V3 Supercharger; AC at home typically 11 kW.
charging.byTypeTooltip:
Breakdown of sessions by charging technology.

AC: Alternating current (home, wallbox, slow)
DC: Direct current (fast chargers, Supercharger)
charging.freeTooltip: This charge is marked as free and is excluded from billing.
charging.rateTooltip: Adjust the charging rate for this session individually (overrides the default rate)
charging.homeBadgeTooltip: Charged at the home wallbox (detected via Monta)
charging.locationEditTooltip: Click to edit — enter the location manually or assign it to a defined charging location if the vehicle did not report GPS data.
charging.locationEditHint: Enter or assign location manually — see tooltip on each field.
charging.locationDefinedTooltip: Direct assignment to a location defined under "Charging locations". Rate (€/kWh) and position are inherited — cost is recalculated.
charging.locationNameTooltip: Free-form display name — shown in the list and CSV exports. Used when no defined location is selected.
charging.locationLatTooltip: GPS latitude. Once lat and lon are set, the app automatically matches against defined charging locations — a hit within radius (200m default) is assigned and its rate inherited.
charging.locationLonTooltip: GPS longitude. A comma in the input is treated as a dot. Useful when the vehicle does not provide a GPS position and you want to map the rate to a known location.
charging.energyAddedTooltip: Actually added energy. Can be slightly lower than the energy delivered by the charger due to charging losses.
charging.socTooltip: Battery state of charge before and after the session
charging.chargerTypeAC: AC charging – at home or public wallbox. Typically 3.7–22 kW. Gentle on the battery.
charging.chargerTypeDC: DC fast charging – motorway fast chargers. Typically 50–350 kW. Slightly more demanding on the battery.
charging.chargerTypeTesla: Tesla Supercharger – V2 up to 150 kW, V3 up to 250 kW. Usually cheaper than other DC fast chargers.
charging.chargerTypeCombo: CCS Combo – European DC fast-charging standard. Up to 350 kW.
charging.chargerTypeUnknown: Charging technology could not be determined

battery.title: Battery & Degradation
battery.noData: Not enough data for degradation analysis yet
battery.noDataLong: Not enough data for degradation analysis yet. At least two measurements are required.
battery.tooltipRangeHistory: Maximum range of the vehicle (at 100% SoC) over time. Decreases slowly – that is battery degradation.
battery.tooltipFirst: Oldest range measurement stored in the database. The further back, the more meaningful the degradation reading.
battery.tooltipLast: Most recent range measurement. Saved by the poller every 15 minutes while the vehicle is online.
battery.tooltipDegradation:
Percent loss of maximum range since the first measurement.

Green (<5%): Normal wear
Yellow (5–10%): Elevated, but not critical
Red (>10%): Significant aging
battery.range30: Short-term trend of the last 30 days – shows acute fluctuations
battery.range90: Quarterly trend over the last 3 months – default view
battery.range180: Half-year trend – shows seasonal variations (winter vs. summer)
battery.range365: Yearly trend – most reliable indicator of battery aging

control.title: 🎮 Vehicle control
control.sleepHintDetail: Commands are retried automatically. If needed, press "Wake up" first (~30s).
control.preConditionTooltip: Maximum heating power for fast preconditioning (battery + windows)
control.keeperOffTooltip: Climate Keeper off — climate stops when you leave the car.
control.keeperKeepTooltip: Keep climate — interior stays at target temperature even when the driver leaves.
control.keeperDogTooltip: Dog mode — climate holds at 20°C, the display shows a notice for passers-by plus the current cabin temperature.
control.keeperCampTooltip: Camp mode — for overnight stays: climate, USB power and cabin lights stay active.
control.steeringWheelTooltip: Toggle the steering wheel heater. Climate must be active.
control.seatHeaterHint: Climate must be running for seat heaters to work.
control.trunkTooltip: Toggle the rear trunk — only on models with a powered tailgate.
control.chargeAmpsTooltip: Maximum charging current on the wallbox (5–48 A). Lower = gentler, slower; higher = faster, more heat.
control.chargePortOpenTooltip: Open the charge port (Tesla and CCS sockets). Often helps to release a locked plug.
control.chargePortCloseTooltip: Close the charge port — only works while no plug is inserted.
control.boomboxHint: Plays Tesla sounds through the external speakers (per Tesla API). Vehicle must be stationary — ignored while driving.
control.boomboxHornTooltip: Standard horn — same as the regular honk, but without flashing lights.
control.departureHint: Tesla preconditions the cabin + battery to be ready at the scheduled departure time. Only works while the vehicle is plugged in.
control.departureTimeTooltip: Time at which the car should be ready to drive. Tesla typically starts preconditioning 20–30 minutes earlier.
control.departureWeekdaysTooltip: If active, preconditioning only runs on weekdays — useful for commuters.
control.departureSaveTooltip: Send the departure schedule to the vehicle.
control.offPeakHint: Delay charging start to a fixed time — useful for dynamic tariffs (Tibber, aWattar, …) or off-peak contracts. The car then charges from that time until the charge limit is reached.
control.offPeakStartTooltip: Time at which the car begins charging. Plug it in beforehand — it waits silently until the scheduled time.
control.offPeakSaveTooltip: Send the scheduled charge start to the vehicle.
control.offPeakDisableTooltip: Disable off-peak plan — the car charges immediately when plugged in.
control.swRefreshTooltip: Re-fetch the status from the vehicle.
control.swInstallTooltip: Schedule the update immediately (starts in 1 minute). Vehicle must be parked and plugged in.
control.navHint: Send a destination directly to the vehicle — opens navigation in the car.
control.toastWakeTimeout: Timeout – the vehicle is not responding
control.toastAsleep: Vehicle is asleep – wake it first

exportPage.title: Export data
exportPage.tripsDesc: All trip data with distance, consumption and SoC
exportPage.backupDesc: All data (trips, charging, battery, logbook) as one JSON file
exportPage.notificationsDesc: Receive a browser notification when your car has finished charging

maintenanceLog.title: Service log
maintenanceLog.addTooltip: Add a new entry to the service log — e.g. maintenance, repair or note
maintenanceLog.filterAllTooltip: Show all entries regardless of category
maintenanceLog.empty: No entries yet. Create your first service-log entry!
maintenanceLog.entryTitleTooltip: Short, descriptive label of the event (e.g. "Summer tyres 2026")
maintenanceLog.dateTooltip: When did it happen? Default is "now"; backdated and forward-dated entries are allowed.
maintenanceLog.categoryTooltip: Type of entry — makes filtering easier later
maintenanceLog.descriptionTooltip: Optional: detailed notes, work performed, workshop etc.
maintenanceLog.odometerTooltip: Current odometer reading at the time of the entry
maintenanceLog.costTooltip: Optional: cost of this maintenance/repair — helps with the total cost calculation
maintenanceLog.creator: Author of this entry
maintenanceLog.creatorUnknown: Author unknown — entry from before the author-tracking feature was added
maintenanceLog.dateInlineTooltip: Date and time of the action
maintenanceLog.mileageInlineTooltip: Odometer reading at the time of the entry
maintenanceLog.cat_note_tooltip: General note or observation about the vehicle
maintenanceLog.cat_maintenance_tooltip: Planned maintenance (brake fluid, cabin filter, wiper blades etc.)
maintenanceLog.cat_tires_tooltip: Tyre change, purchase, pressure checks, tyre repair
maintenanceLog.cat_accident_tooltip: Accident damage and the associated repairs
maintenanceLog.cat_other_tooltip: Other events that don't fit any of the categories

billing.title: Home charging billing
billing.notCompany: This vehicle is configured as a private vehicle

settings.manualTooltip: Opens Tesla's owner's manual for this model in your language (tesla.com)
settings.title: Settings
settings.privateDesc: Private trips only, no logbook required
settings.montaClientIdHint: Partner API: Hub → Application → Client ID
settings.montaKeyHint: Partner API: Hub → Application → Client Secret

abrp.sectionTip: A Better Route Planner (ABRP) receives your Tesla's live data — battery level, speed, position — to calculate more precise routes and charging stops. The token is stored securely and only transmitted while driving.
abrp.tokenTooltip: Find your personal ABRP token in the ABRP app or website: Profile → Live Data → Connect Third-Party App
abrp.tokenHint: ABRP app or website: Profile → Live Data → Connect Third-Party App → show token
abrp.activeHint: ABRP active — live telemetry is being transmitted

settings.driversHint: Create and name drivers – assigned to trips and shown in reports.
settings.defaultTooltip: Default driver – auto-assigned to new trips
settings.deleteDriverConfirm: Delete driver "{name}"? Existing trips will be set to no driver.
settings.mfaNotEnabled: MFA is not enabled. Enable it for more security.
settings.passkeyDesc: Register a passkey for this device to log in without a password.
settings.virtualKeyDesc: Register once on the vehicle so climate, doors and charging work
settings.telemetryDesc: Streams GPS track, speed and battery live to this app
settings.themeDesc: Accent colour for buttons and navigation. Saved locally.
settings.navHint: Change order with ↑↓ · Eye to show/hide
settings.languageHint: Your display language. Synced to your profile.
settings.tenantDefaultLocaleHint: Suggested to new users and signed-out sessions.
settings.pseudonymIntro: On the public login page your tenant appears under this pseudonym — not the real name. That way no outsider can tell which person or company is using this self-hosted instance.
settings.pseudonymRegenerateTip: Generates a new pseudonym — every authorised user has to memorise the new one
settings.pseudonymWarnUsers: All users in your tenant must memorise the new pseudonym — inform them beforehand!
settings.pseudonymWarnHistory: The old pseudonym is moved to history and won't be suggested again by chance.
settings.pseudonymWarnBackup: If you lose the new name without a backup, recovery means starting from a blank tenant environment — all data would be lost.
settings.teslaStrategyTitle: Strategy: live streaming preferred, polling as fallback
settings.teslaStrategyTelemetry: Fleet Telemetry (push) — the car streams GPS, speed, battery every second. Free, no Tesla API calls. Activate per VIN below once Tesla approves.
settings.teslaStrategyPolling: Fleet API polling (pull) — when Telemetry is inactive, the poller hits the Tesla API every 30s (online) or 5min (offline). Costs API budget per call. Currently the active default.
settings.teslaStrategyFooter: Once Telemetry streams for a vehicle, the poller switches that car into heartbeat mode (1× per hour, master data only) — saves up to 95 % of the API budget.
settings.telemetryIntro: Pushes position, speed and battery every second from the car to this app. Requires Tesla partner approval per application client.
settings.telemetryStatusIdle: Registered — waiting for a signal from the car
settings.telemetryStatusApproval: Tesla approval missing — request in the Developer Portal
settings.geofenceHint: Set Home and Work locations. New trips are auto-classified as Private (home→home), Commute (home↔work) or Business (work→work).
settings.geofenceEmpty: No geofences yet. Set at least 'Home' to enable auto-classification.
settings.gpsFuzzingIntro: When active, new trip start/end coordinates are rounded to an approximate location. Existing trips remain untouched. GPS tracks on the map stay precise — only the aggregated start/end pin is fuzzed.
settings.gpsFuzzingEnable: Enable GPS fuzzing for new trips
settings.gpsFuzzingRadiusHint: 50 m = neighbourhood · 200 m = block · 1000 m = district
settings.passkeyTooltip: Passkeys enable passwordless login via Touch ID, Face ID or security key.
settings.passkeyAddTooltip: Browser will prompt — confirm with fingerprint, face recognition or security key

auth.selfHostHint: Open source · Run your own instance for private use — {link}
auth.qrHint: Scan the QR code with your smartphone and confirm with Passkey or Face ID.

register.title: Register new tenant
register.noInvite: Please contact an administrator to receive an invitation link
register.subtitle: Each tenant has a fully isolated database.
register.tenantSlugHint: Lowercase letters, digits and -. Required for sign-in.
register.inviteRequired: Registration requires a valid invitation link.
register.inviteCheckFailed: Invitation link could not be verified.
register.errSlugFormat: Slug: lowercase letters, digits and - only
register.errPwTooShort: Password must be at least 12 characters

mfa.step2Hint: Enter the 6-digit code from your app
mfa.backupOnce: These codes are shown only once

setup.title: Initial setup – create admin account
setup.subtitle: Initial setup – create admin account
setup.notConfigured: Tesla Carview has not been set up yet
setup.wizardDesc: This wizard creates your first administrator account
setup.wizardDescLong: Tesla Carview has not been set up yet. This wizard creates your first administrator account.
setup.featureLocal: ✓ All vehicle data stays locally on your server
setup.adminDesc: This account has full access to the application
setup.adminDescDot: This account has full access to the application.
setup.tenantHint: Only relevant if you run multiple tenants
setup.tenantHintLong: Only relevant if you run multiple tenants. Default: "Default".
setup.doneDesc: Your administrator account has been created successfully
setup.pseudonymExplain: For privacy reasons, your tenant does not appear on the login page with its real name but with this pseudonym. That way nobody from outside can tell which company or person uses this self-hosted instance.
setup.pseudonymWarn: ⚠ Please memorise or note it down safely. With multiple tenants, the pseudonym is your only identifier on the login page.
setup.pseudonymBackup: 💾 Create a backup now (Settings → Data → Download backup). If you lose the pseudonym without a backup, recovery is only possible by setting up an empty tenant from scratch — all data would be lost.
setup.pseudonymRegen: The admin can regenerate the pseudonym later (Settings → Regenerate tenant pseudonym).
setup.ns3: ③ Change password after first login
setup.nsPseudonym: ① Note pseudonym safely + create backup
setup.errShort: Password must be at least 12 characters

users.title: User management
users.inviteDesc: Only those with this link can register a new tenant
users.pwTooShort: Password must be at least 12 characters

data.title: Data management
data.deleteAllDesc: All trips, charging sessions, telemetry, logbook and audit logs
data.deleteVehicleDesc: Deletes all data for a single vehicle
data.deleteSuccess: All data deleted. Backup was created

system.title: 📊 System & Version
system.noUpdate: Up to date – you are running the latest build

delete.title: Delete tenant permanently?
delete.warning: All data of this tenant will be removed — vehicles, trips, charging sessions, users.
delete.backupNote: A backup copy of the database file is created automatically before deletion.
delete.typeSlug: Type the tenant slug to confirm:

overview.title: 🌟 Overview
overview.body: Tesla Carview is a self-hosted data logger app for electric vehicles. All data stays exclusively on your own server – no cloud, no data sharing. The app is fully responsive and runs on iPhone/iPad (Safari), Android smartphones and desktop browsers.

features.title: Trip log
features.desc: GPS tracks, consumption, trip type classification

features.title: Charging
features.desc: Charging sessions with costs, GPS location detection

features.title: Control
features.desc: Climate, doors, lights – directly from the app

features.title: Export
features.desc: CSV/JSON for all data, full backup as ZIP

features.title: Push notifications
features.desc: Browser notification when charging is complete

features.title: Mobile-optimised
features.desc: Fully usable on iPhone/iPad (Safari), Android and desktop

requirements.title: 📋 Requirements
serverItems: Linux server (x86_64, ARM64 or ARMv7) with min. 1 GB RAM
Docker + Docker Compose (installed by setup script)
Publicly accessible domain + TLS certificate (required for API)
Port 443 (HTTPS) must be reachable from outside
teslaItems: Create app → note Client ID and Client Secret
For vehicle commands: apply for Fleet API Access (free, 1–3 business days)

installation.title: 🚀 Installation
installation.desc: The setup script installs everything automatically: Docker, nginx, TLS, tesla-http-proxy.
installation.asRoot: # As root on the target server:
installation.promptClientId: → Tesla Client ID and Client Secret
installation.altDesc: Copy .env.example → .env and adjust all values. Then: docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d

steps.title: Open browser
steps.desc: Open https://<your-domain>/setup — you will be redirected automatically.

steps.title: Create tenant
steps.desc: Choose a tenant name (e.g. "Smith Family") and a slug (e.g. "smith"). The slug is required at login – note it down.

steps.title: Create admin account
steps.desc: Set username and password. Password must be at least 12 characters. Recommendation: a passphrase of 4 words.

steps.title: Connect vehicle
steps.desc: After login: Settings → Connect Tesla account. You will be redirected to Tesla to sign in.

steps.title: Import vehicles
steps.desc: Go to Settings → Vehicle connection → Sync vehicles. All vehicles of the connected account are imported automatically.

virtualKey.title: 🔑 Setting up the Virtual Key
virtualKey.desc: For vehicle commands (unlock, climate etc.) a Virtual Key must be registered on the vehicle.
steps: Make sure tesla-http-proxy is running: systemctl status tesla-http-proxy
The Tesla app asks 'Allow app?' → confirm
Stay within Bluetooth range of the vehicle — the key is accepted within 30 seconds
Verify under Settings → Vehicle connection → Virtual Key status

chargingLocations.title: ⚡ Charging locations & costs
chargingLocations.desc: Charging locations are detected automatically via GPS and linked to a price per kWh.
chargingLocations.autoDesc: When a charging location with GPS coordinates and radius (default 200m) is stored, the correct location is detected automatically at the start of charging and the stored price/kWh is applied.
chargingLocations.createDesc: Under Charging → Locations: enter name, type (home/office/public), price/kWh, GPS coordinates and detection radius.
chargingLocations.adjustDesc: In the charging list: click a session → edit costs. Costs can also be set to 0 (e.g. free charging).
chargingLocations.freeDesc: Each session has a small '✕ mark free' button. Sessions marked this way appear greyed out with a 'free' badge and are automatically excluded from the home charging billing.
chargingLocations.freeHint: Typical use case: charging at the workplace provided by the employer. Use the '↩ mark paid' button to undo the marking at any time.

security.title: 🔐 Security

features.title: Passkey / WebAuthn
features.desc: Passwordless login with fingerprint, Face ID or hardware key

features.title: Account lockout
features.desc: Account locked after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes

features.title: Refresh token
features.desc: httpOnly cookie, valid 7 days, automatic rotation

features.title: Audit log
features.desc: All logins, changes and security events logged

features.title: HTTPS + HSTS
features.desc: TLS 1.2/1.3, HSTS, OCSP stapling, secure headers

recommend: Set up a passkey for passwordless login
Strong password: min. 16 characters or 4-word passphrase

multitenancy.title: 🏢 Multi-tenancy
multitenancy.desc: Tesla Carview supports multiple fully isolated tenants on one instance. Each tenant has its own database – no tenant can ever see another's data.
multitenancy.inviteDesc: New tenants can only be registered via an invitation link. An administrator generates the link under Admin → Users → Create invitation link. The link is valid for 7 days and can only be used once.
multitenancy.multiVehicleDesc: All vehicles of a connected account are imported automatically when syncing. Switch between vehicles in the top-right navigation bar.
multitenancy.loginDesc: With multiple tenants, a tenant field appears at login. With only one tenant, it is detected automatically.
multitenancy.userMgmtDesc: Administrators can create additional users and assign vehicles within their tenant. Under Admin → Users.

backup.title: 💾 Data backup
backup.manualDesc: Under Export: CSV or JSON for trips and charging sessions, and full backup as ZIP.
backup.autoDesc: The SQLite databases are in the Docker volume tesla_data. For automatic server backups:
backup.warningDesc: Always create an export before deleting data. Deleted data cannot be recovered.

teslaApi.title: ⚡ Tesla Developer API setup
teslaApi.desc: Tesla Carview communicates via the official Tesla Fleet API. You need a free Tesla Developer account and a registered app.
step1Items: Visit developer.tesla.com and sign in with your Tesla account.
Accept the Developer Terms of Service.
step2Items: Application Name: any name, e.g. Tesla Carview
Allowed Origin: your public app URL
Scopes (required): vehicle_device_data, vehicle_cmds, vehicle_charging_cmds, vehicle_location, openid, offline_access
⚠ vehicle_location is required for GPS tracking (Fleet Telemetry)
step3Items: Client Secret – visible only once, copy and store securely
teslaApi.step3Hint: Enter these values in the .env file or provide them in the interactive setup wizard.
teslaApi.step4Title: Step 4 – Apply for Fleet API Access (for commands)
teslaApi.step4Desc: For vehicle commands (climate, doors, charging) your app must be registered as a partner with Tesla.
teslaApi.step4Hint: The setup script runs this step automatically when FRONTEND_URL is set. Otherwise manually via Postman or curl. Takes 1–3 business days to activate.
step5Items: After login: Settings → Vehicle connection → Reconnect
You will be redirected to Tesla to sign in and grant the app access.
All vehicles appear in the app.
teslaApi.step6Desc: GPS data on newer vehicles (e.g. Model Y from 2024, XP7 VIN) comes exclusively via Fleet Telemetry — not the REST API.
step6Steps: Register app with Tesla – Settings → Fleet Telemetry → '🔑 Register app'. One-time only.
Apply for Fleet Telemetry Access – If the next step returns HTTP 404, Tesla hasn't enabled the endpoint yet. Contact Tesla Developer Support.
Activate telemetry – Settings → Fleet Telemetry → '📡 Activate telemetry'.
teslaApi.supportTitle: Requesting Fleet Telemetry Access from Tesla Support
teslaApi.supportDesc: If step 2 returns 404, send the following request to the Tesla Developer Support form (developer.tesla.com/dashboard → Support Inquiry):
teslaApi.supportNote: ⚠ Replace Client ID and VIN with your own values. Tesla typically responds within a few days.

monta.title: 🔌 Monta integration (home charging billing)
monta.desc: For company car drivers: Tesla Carview can pull charging data directly from your Monta wallbox and create a monthly cost statement for your employer.
monta.warning: ⚠️ Monta integration is only available for vehicles in the Company car category (Settings → Vehicle profile → Category).
monta.step1Title: Step 1 – Create Monta Partner API credentials
step1Items: Log in to the Monta Operator Hub (hub.monta.com).
Go to Settings → Applications → Create new application.
Copy both Client ID and Client Secret – both are required in Tesla Carview.
monta.step1Hint: The Client Secret is only visible once – enter it in Tesla Carview immediately.
step2Items: In the Monta portal: Charge points → My devices.
The Charge Point ID is in the detail view (format: cp_12345).
Alternative: API call GET /api/v1/charge-points returns all charge points with IDs.
step3Items: Add Monta charge point ID and Monta API key.
useItems: Go to Billing in the navigation.
Select the desired month – all home charging sessions are listed.
Sessions that were free can be marked with ✕ mark free in the charging history – they are excluded from billing.
PDF export gives you a ready-to-sign billing sheet.

items.a: Newer models (XP7 VIN, e.g. Model Y Juniper) do not provide drive_state via REST API. GPS tracking uses Fleet Telemetry. Make sure tesla-http-proxy is running and the Virtual Key is registered.
After updating to v2.0 (multi-tenant), a tenant slug is required at login. The slug for the migrated database is 'default'. Enter it in the login field or click 'Select tenant'.
The Tesla OAuth token has expired. Go to Settings → Vehicle connection and reconnect. Make sure TESLA_CLIENT_ID and TESLA_CLIENT_SECRET in the .env are correct.
Check: 1) tesla-http-proxy is running (systemctl status tesla-http-proxy) 2) Virtual Key is registered on the vehicle 3) Vehicle is online (not sleeping).

Fleet Telemetry requires two steps: (1) register app with Tesla, (2) activate telemetry. If step 2 returns HTTP 404, fleet_telemetry_config access must be requested from Tesla Developer Support. Also ensure vehicle_location is enabled in the app scopes on developer.tesla.com.
Check logs: docker logs tesla-carview-backend. Common causes: missing .env variables (JWT_SECRET, TESLA_CLIENT_ID), database migration error.

donate.title: ❤️ If the app is worth something to you
donate.desc: Tesla Carview is free and ad-free for private, self-hosted use (license: PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 — commercial resale or SaaS hosting for third parties is not permitted). If you'd like to give something back, these organisations would appreciate your support.
donate.footer: 100% of your donation goes directly to the organisation. We see neither the amount nor your data.

orgs.name: Aktion Deutschland Hilft
orgs.desc: Alliance of aid organisations for rapid and effective disaster relief worldwide.

orgs.name: Lebenshilfe Rems-Murr
orgs.desc: Support, accompaniment and participation for people with disabilities in the Rems-Murr district.

orgs.name: Radio 7 Drachenkinder
orgs.desc: Help for seriously ill children in the region – funds therapies, outings and wishes.

teslaUsage.title: Tesla API usage
teslaUsage.subtitle: Projected Tesla Fleet API costs for the current month.
teslaUsage.hardStopOn: Hard-stop active – further calls are blocked

footer.contactPrompt: Commercial inquiries or IT-design & architecture consulting?

legal.placeholdersWarningAdmin: This page still contains unresolved placeholders. Please fill them in via the admin editor before going live:
legal.acceptCheckboxPrivacy: I have read and accept the {link}.
legal.acceptCheckboxTerms: I have read and accept the {link}.
legal.acceptModalBody: Privacy Policy and/or Terms of Use have been updated. Please confirm the new versions before continuing.
legal.adminIntro: Maintain legal notice, privacy policy and terms of use per language. When saving, tick "Bump version" if the change is material — all users must then re-accept on next login.
legal.adminSyncModeHint: Recommended. When you save, the German text is copied 1:1 into all other languages. The public view shows a banner that this page is currently only maintained in German.
legal.syncedFromDefaultBanner: This page is currently only maintained in German — what you see here is the German source text.

invite.title: Accept invitation
invite.subtitle: Create your account in {tenant}. Role: {role}
invite.errUnknown: This invitation link is unknown — the administrator may have revoked it.
invite.errAlreadyUsed: This invitation link has already been used. Ask the administrator for a new one.
invite.errExpired: This invitation link has expired. Ask the administrator for a new one.
invite.errGeneric: The link could not be verified.
invite.errFormIncomplete: Please fill in all fields and accept the privacy policy and terms.
invite.doneText: Hello {username} — your account in {tenant} was created. You can now log in.

grok.title: Grok Chat
grok.placeholder: Ask Grok something about your trips, charging data or your Tesla …
grok.budgetExceeded: Daily budget reached — available again tomorrow.
grok.emptyState: No chats yet — start a new one.
grok.welcomeHint: Start a new chat and ask Grok about your Tesla data.
grok.budgetTooltip: Cost today / daily budget in cents. Resets at midnight.
grok.errorBillingBody: Your xAI team has no credits or licence yet. Add at least $5 to your xAI account – Grok Chat will work immediately afterwards.

routes.title: Route Planner
routes.clickMapHint: Or click on the map to set a destination.
routes.noWaypoints: No waypoints. Add them via the search field.
routes.sendTooltip: Sends the destination address directly to the vehicle navigation system.
routes.abrpTooltip: Opens A Better Route Planner with live telemetry. May require a subscription.
routes.asleep: Vehicle is asleep — please wake it up first.
routes.chargerTooltip: Shows fast-charging stations (DC) along the route — free via OpenChargeMap.
routes.noResults: No results found. Try a more specific query.
routes.startVehicleTip: Use current vehicle location as starting point
routes.startBrowserTip: Use device GPS as starting point
routes.startNoLocation: No start location set — vehicle position unknown.
routes.chargingPlanHint: Calculate optimal charging stops along the route.
routes.noStopsNeeded: No charging stop needed — range sufficient
routes.planNotFeasible: Range insufficient — route not drivable without charging
routes.autoSendLabel: Auto-send to Tesla 15 min before departure
routes.modeDepartTip: Set departure time – arrival is calculated
routes.modeArriveTip: Set desired arrival time – required departure is calculated
routes.weatherHint: Weather is loaded after route calculation.
routes.camerasTooltip: Show speed camera warnings from OpenStreetMap (free, incomplete)
routes.camerasLegalText: Speed camera warning apps may be illegal while driving in some countries. Please check local regulations. Use at your own risk.

wizard.title: Settings Wizard
wizard.intro: Personalize the app in a few simple steps.
wizard.featureDraft: Changes are only saved at the end
wizard.featureBack: Go back and correct at any time
wizard.featureRelaunch: Wizard can be restarted anytime from Settings

s2.title: Design Style
s2.question: Which visual style should the app use?

s3.title: Accent Color
s3.question: Which accent color do you prefer?

s5.title: Dashboard
s5.question: Which cards should appear on the dashboard and in what order?

s6.title: Navigation
s6.question: Which menu items do you want to see?

s7.title: Notifications
s7.question: When do you want a push notification?

s8.title: Summary
s8.question: Review your changes and confirm.
s8.noChanges: No settings were changed — all values remain as before.

pair.title: Sign in device via QR code
pair.invalid: This QR code is invalid or has expired.
pair.invalidHint: Please generate a new QR code in the login form.
pair.alreadyConfirmed: This session has already been confirmed.
pair.noPasskeyHint: This device does not support passkey authentication. Please open on a device with Face ID or fingerprint sensor.
pair.readyHint: Tap the button and confirm with Face ID, Touch ID or your security key.
pair.successHint: The other device will be logged in automatically.
